assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend gives rise to a recognised intragroup receivable/payable denominated in a foreign currency.
The question concerns consolidated financial statements and a documented hedge relationship under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk of a recognised item or highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for the foreign currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting for exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items, including intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements, outside hedge accounting.
